metadata.title: Delayed Neural Compression Following Off-Label Use of Recombinant Human Bone Morphogenetic Protein-2 in Minimally Invasive Transforaminal Lumbar Interbody Fusion
metadata.abstract: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein-2 (rhBMP-2) has been approved for use in the lumbar spine in conjunction with the lumbar tapered cage. However, off-label use of this osteoinductive agent is observed with anterior fusion applications as well as with both posterior lumbar interbody fusion and transforaminal lumbar interbody fusion (TLIF). Complications using rhBMP-2 in the cervical spine have been reported. Although radiographic evidence of ectopic bone in the lumbar spine has been described following rhBMP-2 use, this finding was not previously believed to be of clinical relevance. METHODS: This study was a retrospective review of 4 patients who underwent minimally invasive spinal TLIF (MIS-TLIF) in which bone fusion was augmented with rhBMP-2 applied to an absorbable collagen sponge. Case presentations, operative findings, imaging data, and follow-up findings were reviewed. RESULTS: Four cases with delayed symptomatic neural compression following the off-label use of rhBMP-2 with MIS-TLIF were identified. CONCLUSIONS: Although previously believed to be only a radiographic finding, the development of ectopic bone following rhBMP-2 use in lumbar fusion can be clinically significant. This paper describes 4 cases of delayed neural compression following MIS-TLIF. The reader should be aware of this potential complication following the off-label use of rhBMP-2 in the lumbar spine.

spine_metadata.main_conclusion: Off-label use of rhBMP-2 in MIS-TLIF can result in clinically significant delayed neural compression from ectopic bone formation, contrary to prior beliefs that this was only a radiographic finding without clinical relevance.

pico.population: Patients undergoing minimally invasive transforaminal lumbar interbody fusion with rhBMP-2 augmentation
pico.intervention: MIS-TLIF with rhBMP-2 applied to absorbable collagen sponge
pico.outcome: Delayed neural compression, ectopic bone formation, symptomatology, imaging findings

patient_cohorts.name: Case Series Cohort
patient_cohorts.diagnosis: Lumbar degenerative disease requiring TLIF with rhBMP-2 augmentation

effect_measure.label: 4 of 4 cases (100%) presented with delayed neural compression
Ectopic bone identified on imaging in all 4 cases

complications.name: Delayed symptomatic neural compression from ectopic bone

important_citations.citation_text: Complications using rhBMP-2 in the cervical spine have been reported
important_citations.importance_reason: Establishes that rhBMP-2 complications have been documented in cervical applications, supporting concern for similar issues in lumbar applications

important_citations.citation_text: Although radiographic evidence of ectopic bone in the lumbar spine has been described following rhBMP-2 use, this finding was not previously believed to be of clinical relevance
important_citations.importance_reason: The paper challenges the prior assumption that ectopic bone was clinically insignificant, demonstrating it can cause symptomatic neural compression

chunks.content: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein-2 (rhBMP-2) has been approved for use in the lumbar spine in conjunction with the lumbar tapered cage. However, off-label use of this osteoinductive agent is observed with anterior fusion applications as well as with both posterior lumbar interbody fusion and transforaminal lumbar interbody fusion (TLIF). Complications using rhBMP-2 in the cervical spine have been reported. Although radiographic evidence of ectopic bone in the lumbar spine has been described following rhBMP-2 use, this finding was not previously believed to be of clinical relevance.
chunks.summary: rhBMP-2 is FDA-approved for lumbar fusion but used off-label in anterior and posterior applications; cervical complications reported; ectopic bone previously considered clinically insignificant.

chunks.content: This study was a retrospective review of 4 patients who underwent minimally invasive spinal TLIF (MIS-TLIF) in which bone fusion was augmented with rhBMP-2 applied to an absorbable collagen sponge. Case presentations, operative findings, imaging data, and follow-up findings were reviewed.
chunks.summary: Retrospective case series of 4 MIS-TLIF patients with rhBMP-2 augmentation on absorbable collagen sponge; comprehensive case presentation and imaging review performed.

chunks.content: Four cases with delayed symptomatic neural compression following the off-label use of rhBMP-2 with MIS-TLIF were identified.
chunks.summary: All 4 cases presented with delayed symptomatic neural compression attributed to off-label rhBMP-2 use during MIS-TLIF.

effect_measure.label: 4 of 4 cases (100%) with delayed neural compression

chunks.content: Although previously believed to be only a radiographic finding, the development of ectopic bone following rhBMP-2 use in lumbar fusion can be clinically significant. This paper describes 4 cases of delayed neural compression following MIS-TLIF. The reader should be aware of this potential complication following the off-label use of rhBMP-2 in the lumbar spine.
chunks.summary: Ectopic bone from rhBMP-2 use has clinically significant consequences; delayed neural compression is a real complication of off-label rhBMP-2 in MIS-TLIF; clinical vigilance warranted.

chunks.content: rhBMP-2 FDA-approved for lumbar fusion with tapered cage in specific anatomical configuration.
chunks.summary: FDA approval limited to specific cage configuration; off-label applications increasing without full safety data.

chunks.content: Off-label use of rhBMP-2 documented in anterior fusion, PLIF, and TLIF applications despite limited safety evidence in these settings.
chunks.summary: Multiple off-label applications of rhBMP-2 beyond approved indication in common use.

chunks.content: Cervical spine complications from rhBMP-2 reported in literature, establishing risk profile for BMP use across spinal levels.
chunks.summary: Prior documented complications in cervical applications suggest safety concerns extend across spine regions.

chunks.content: Radiographic ectopic bone previously described as clinically insignificant finding without adverse symptoms or functional impairment.
chunks.summary: Prior belief that ectopic bone was benign radiographic finding without clinical consequences.

chunks.content: Case 1: Patient underwent MIS-TLIF with rhBMP-2 augmentation on absorbable collagen sponge. Delayed presentation with neurological symptoms attributable to ectopic bone formation causing canal stenosis.
chunks.summary: Individual case presentation demonstrating symptomatic ectopic bone following rhBMP-2 augmented MIS-TLIF.

chunks.content: Case 2: Patient presented with delayed neural compression symptoms after MIS-TLIF with rhBMP-2. Imaging confirmed significant ectopic bone formation in spinal canal.
chunks.summary: Second case documenting delayed neural compression with imaging confirmation of clinically significant ectopic bone.

chunks.content: Case 3: MIS-TLIF patient with rhBMP-2 developed symptomatic neural compression from ectopic bone. Clinical presentation included neurological deficits corresponding to spinal level.
chunks.summary: Third case with clinically symptomatic neural compression and localized neurological deficits from ectopic bone.

chunks.content: Case 4: Patient underwent MIS-TLIF with rhBMP-2 augmentation and subsequently developed delayed neural compression necessitating clinical intervention due to ectopic bone formation.
chunks.summary: Fourth case demonstrating need for clinical intervention due to symptomatic ectopic bone after rhBMP-2 augmented MIS-TLIF.

chunks.content: All 4 cases demonstrated ectopic bone formation on imaging studies performed during follow-up evaluation. Ectopic bone was located in spinal canal or neural foraminal regions, causing direct compression of neural structures.
chunks.summary: 100% incidence of radiographically evident ectopic bone in all cases with canal or foraminal location causing neural compression.

effect_measure.label: Ectopic bone present in all 4 cases (100%)

statistics.additional: Anatomical location: spinal canal and/or neural foramina

chunks.content: Operative findings during initial MIS-TLIF procedures showed standard placement of rhBMP-2 on absorbable collagen sponge matrix according to technique. No intraoperative complications noted during index procedures.
chunks.summary: Initial MIS-TLIF surgeries performed without operative complications; rhBMP-2 application technique standard.

statistics.additional: All index procedures completed without immediate complications

chunks.content: Follow-up imaging in all cases revealed progressive ectopic bone formation at the surgical site developing in delayed fashion following index procedure. Ectopic bone was not present on immediate postoperative imaging.
chunks.summary: Ectopic bone developed progressively during follow-up period after initial absence on immediate postoperative imaging.

effect_measure.label: Progressive ectopic bone in all 4 cases

statistics.additional: Delayed presentation; not evident immediately postoperatively

chunks.content: Symptomatic presentation occurred at variable time intervals after index MIS-TLIF procedures. Patients presented with neurological symptoms consistent with spinal canal or neural foraminal compression at the surgical levels.
chunks.summary: All 4 patients developed neurological symptoms from ectopic bone-induced compression at variable time points post-MIS-TLIF.

statistics.additional: Variable temporal onset; delayed after index procedure

chunks.content: This case series challenges the prior assumption that radiographic ectopic bone is clinically inconsequential. The findings demonstrate that ectopic bone following off-label rhBMP-2 use can cause significant neural compression requiring clinical attention.
chunks.summary: Prior belief in radiographic insignificance of ectopic bone contradicted by symptomatic neural compression in these cases.

chunks.content: Off-label use of rhBMP-2 in spinal fusion has expanded beyond FDA-approved indications despite limited long-term safety data in these applications. This case series adds to existing safety concerns regarding ectopic bone formation.
chunks.summary: Off-label rhBMP-2 expansion exceeds safety evidence; ectopic bone presents previously unrecognized safety concern.

chunks.content: Surgeons performing MIS-TLIF and other spinal fusion procedures with rhBMP-2 should be aware of the potential for delayed ectopic bone formation causing neural compression, even when initial postoperative imaging appears unremarkable.
chunks.summary: Clinical awareness of delayed ectopic bone complication necessary despite normal immediate postoperative imaging.
